phase_c_status: SKIPPED per pre-reg phase_robustness_followup clause: 'If holdout FAIL outright, skip Phase C.'
structural_note: v3 is the FIRST experiment across 5 in this class (multi_source_two_stage v1+v2+v3 + alt_data v2 + this v3) to produce non-zero Lasso coefficients. The rank-target transformation broke the zero-coef pattern, confirming zen's diagnostic that target-shape (heavy-tailed return distribution) was the bottleneck for the L1+MSE inner solver. However, the holdout outcome is informationally distinct from prior FAILs: signal IS present (positive holdout rank-IC) but inverts under top-N tail selection.

lasso_fit.interpretation: Lasso learned: high short-interest rank predicts LOW future return (Diether-Lee-Werner short-anomaly direction); high filing density predicts HIGH future return. Both signs match published literature on equity short interest and corporate-event activity premia.

fail_reasons: in-CV IR = 0.338 < 0.50 MID gate (zen+perplexity adversarial review concern about fold-instability under rank-objective)
alpha_t_stat = -4.32 (highly significant NEGATIVE, opposite of pass direction)
Top-N selection inverted holdout direction despite positive bulk rank-IC

structural_findings.rank_transform_broke_zero_coef_pattern: First of 5 experiments on this universe to surface non-zero Lasso coefficients. Confirms zen's adversarial-review diagnostic that L1+MSE on heavy-tailed return targets drives lambda to maximum-penalty grid point. Rank-percentile target compresses to bounded [-0.5,+0.5] where modest cross-sectional ordering signal survives L1.
structural_findings.holdout_rank_ic_positive_but_top_n_inver: Holdout mean per-asof rank-IC = +0.0260 — bulk-of-distribution correlation between predictions and forward returns is positive. But top-30 by predicted score produced -76%/y excess net. Mechanism: top-30 selection magnifies tail behavior; the model's predicted-best stocks (low SI + high filings = quality/value-tilted) underperformed catastrophically in the AI-themed 2024-2026 bull market while the broader signal remained directionally correct.
structural_findings.regime_flip_hypothesis: DLW (Diether-Lee-Werner 2009) short-anomaly evidence is from 1988-2002 + post-2008 samples. The 2024-2026 holdout includes the immediate post-zero-rate AI rally where high-short-interest names (e.g. AI-adjacent shorts) heavily outperformed via short squeeze. Train period 2018-2024 included normal-regime short-anomaly behavior; holdout inverted it. Ledger entry documents this as regime-shift evidence, not feature failure.
structural_findings.in_cv_ir_failure_separately: IR = 0.338 means fold-to-fold rank-IC dispersion is high (one fold strongly positive, others weakly so). Even pre-holdout, the signal stability is below the IR=0.5 MID threshold per the pre-reg gate. This was correctly flagged by the new gate before the holdout fail compounded the verdict.

what_v5_settles: Target shape (raw-return vs rank-percentile) is now a SETTLED architectural variable in this universe. Rank-target IS the right transformation (broke zero-coef pattern + surfaced literature-canonical coef signs), but cross-sectional rank-IC at this universe is too low and too unstable across folds to support a top-30 long-only strategy.
what_v5_does_not_settle: Whether different selection rules (e.g. long/short top-30/bottom-30, decile-spread portfolios) would have surfaced positive results given the bulk-of-distribution rank-IC=+0.026. v5's selection rule was held identical to v4 v2 to isolate the target-transform variable; selection-rule ablation is a future variant.

research_program_status.this_was: 12th paradigm failure since 2026-04-25 strategic pivot (ordered: themed/fundamental/insider/rotation/tri-factor/mom+lowvol/quality+momentum/vol-target/multi_source v1/v2/v3/alt_data v2/alt_data v3)

defensible_next_class_options.rationale: v3 demonstrated rank-target IS architecturally correct + features carry weak ordinal signal (+0.026 rank-IC). Bottleneck is selection rule magnifying tail behavior, OR fold-instability indicating signal too weak for top-N strategies. Defensible next-class options:

options.description: Same rank-target Lasso, BUT change selection rule from top-30-long to long-decile-minus-short-decile (or top-30-long minus bottom-30-short, market-neutral). Captures the bulk rank-IC instead of magnifying tail.
options.expected_yield: If rank-IC=+0.026 transfers to decile-spread, modest positive alpha possible. Decile-spread is less tail-sensitive.

options.description: Switch to LightGBM with rank-percentile target. Trees capture interactions Lasso cannot. Risk: 222k rows × 10 features still needs careful regularization.
options.expected_yield: Higher capacity to fit; higher overfitting risk.

options.description: 5-fold or walk-forward-with-rolling-window CV may stabilize IR. v3 used 3 folds and IR=0.338 was below 0.5 MID.
options.expected_yield: Better IR estimate may surface that v3's signal is stable enough; or definitively confirm instability.

options.description: 20d holding with 5d stride creates 75% overlap which inflates HAC variance. Reduce stride to non-overlapping (stride=20, holding=20) for cleaner inference. Cuts holdout n=99 to ~25, but each obs is independent.

v3 had 10 features; signal might be too thin. Add 5-10 more (e.g. analyst-revisions via Yahoo, options-IV-skew via Polygon if accessible, news sentiment via Polygon). Expensive (Polygon options tier or news endpoint).
options.expected_yield: If features were the constraint, more features may help. If selection-rule was the constraint, doesn't help.

ranking_by_information_value: A (selection rule clean isolation, low cost)
